content:
High Court replaces father’s physical visitation with video calls in child custody case

The Gujarat High Court has stayed a Family Court order granting a father temporary physical visitation rights to his minor son and replaced it with virtual visitation through video conferencing. The Court observed that in-person meetings could lead to further disputes between the estranged parents, affecting the child's welfare.

The case involved a couple living separately since 2021 after the birth of their son. The Family Court had permitted the father to meet the child twice a month at a mutually agreed location.

The mother challenged the order, contending that the father had remained largely uninvolved in the child's upbringing, that the mandatory visits disrupted the child's routine, and that, as a working professional, Sundays were her only opportunity to spend quality time with her son.

After considering the circumstances, the High Court observed that the agreed venue for physical meetings had itself become a source of conflict and could trigger further disputes between the parties.

Pending further proceedings, the Court stayed the physical visitation order and directed that the father be allowed to interact with his son through video conferencing on the second and fourth Sundays of every month for two hours. It also directed the mother to cooperate in facilitating the virtual meetings to ensure the child continues to maintain contact with his father.
